name: Bugatti Chiron
shortDescription: Mid-engine hypercar with a quad-turbo W16 engine producing 1,500 hp.
fullDescription: The Bugatti Chiron is a mid-engine two-seater sports car that succeeded the Veyron. Powered by an 8.0-litre quad-turbocharged W16 engine producing 1,500 hp and 1,600 Nm of torque, it is electronically limited to 420 km/h. The chassis is made of carbon fibre, and the suspension is a double wishbone setup at both axles. Only 500 units were planned for production.

name: Lamborghini Aventador
shortDescription: V12-powered flagship supercar with scissor doors and carbon fibre monocoque.
fullDescription: The Aventador is Lamborghini's flagship V12 supercar, featuring a naturally aspirated 6.5L V12. The chassis is a carbon fibre monocoque and the body panels are also carbon fibre. It replaced the Murciélago and was produced until 2022 when the Revuelto took over. The LP 700-4 variant makes 700 hp and uses all-wheel drive.

name: Ford GT40
shortDescription: Le Mans-winning race car that ended Ferrari's dominance in the 1960s.
fullDescription: The Ford GT40 was built to compete at the 24 Hours of Le Mans and defeat Ferrari. After two failed attempts, Ford won Le Mans four consecutive times from 1966 to 1969. The Mk II variant used a 7.0L V8 producing around 485 hp. The name GT40 comes from its height of exactly 40 inches (1,016 mm).

name: Tesla Model S Plaid
shortDescription: Tri-motor all-electric sedan with over 1,000 hp and a sub-2-second 0–60 time.
fullDescription: The Tesla Model S Plaid uses three electric motors (two rear, one front) to produce over 1,020 hp. It features a 100 kWh battery and can travel up to 637 km on a single charge (WLTP). The interior features a large yoke-style steering wheel and a 17-inch horizontal touchscreen. It is one of the fastest-accelerating production cars ever made.

name: Porsche 911 GT3
shortDescription: Naturally aspirated track-focused sports car with a 9,000 rpm flat-six.
fullDescription: The 992-generation GT3 uses a naturally aspirated 4.0L flat-six engine that revs to 9,000 rpm, producing 510 hp. It features a racing-derived double-wishbone front suspension and a rear-axle steering system. The GT3 can lap the Nürburgring Nordschleife in 6:59.927, making it one of the fastest road-legal cars on that circuit.

name: Boeing 747
shortDescription: Wide-body long-range airliner — the iconic Jumbo Jet.
fullDescription: The Boeing 747 is a wide-body commercial jetliner first flown in 1969. Its distinctive upper deck hump gives it an unmistakable silhouette. The 747-8, the latest variant, is the longest commercial aircraft ever built at 76.3 m. More than 1,500 units have been delivered to airlines worldwide. It is powered by four high-bypass turbofan engines.

name: Concorde
shortDescription: Supersonic passenger jet capable of crossing the Atlantic in under 3.5 hours.
fullDescription: Concorde was a turbojet-powered supersonic passenger airliner operated by British Airways and Air France from 1976 to 2003. It cruised at Mach 2.04 (2,179 km/h) at an altitude of 18,300 m. The London–New York route took only 3 hours 30 minutes. Only 20 aircraft were built. The nose drooped 12.5° on landing to give pilots visibility.

name: Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird
shortDescription: Mach 3+ strategic reconnaissance aircraft — the fastest air-breathing jet ever built.
fullDescription: The SR-71 Blackbird is a long-range, high-altitude, Mach 3+ strategic reconnaissance aircraft developed by Lockheed's Skunk Works. It holds the record as the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft, reaching speeds above Mach 3.3 (3,540 km/h). Its airframe was made primarily of titanium to withstand the heat generated by air friction at top speed. The aircraft was operational from 1966 to 1998.

name: F-22 Raptor
shortDescription: 5th-generation stealth air superiority fighter with supercruise capability.
fullDescription: The F-22 Raptor is a fifth-generation stealth fighter combining advanced aerodynamics, supercruise (supersonic flight without afterburner), supermaneuverability, stealth, and sensor fusion. It entered service with the USAF in 2005. The aircraft can cruise at Mach 1.82 without afterburners, and can reach Mach 2+ with them. Its radar cross-section is estimated to be around 0.0001 m².

name: LZ 129 Hindenburg
shortDescription: The largest aircraft ever built — a hydrogen-filled rigid airship for transatlantic travel.
fullDescription: The LZ 129 Hindenburg was a large German commercial passenger-carrying rigid airship. At 245 m it remains the largest aircraft ever built by volume. It flew transatlantic routes between Germany and North America. On May 6, 1937, it caught fire and was destroyed while attempting to dock at Lakehurst, New Jersey, killing 36 of the 97 people on board.

name: LZ 127 Graf Zeppelin
shortDescription: First aircraft to complete a passenger-carrying circumnavigation of the globe.
fullDescription: The LZ 127 Graf Zeppelin was a German hydrogen-filled rigid airship that made the first circumnavigation of the Earth by an aircraft carrying passengers in 1929. It completed 590 flights, travelling over 1.7 million km in its 9-year operational life. It used a unique blau gas fuel system which had approximately the same density as air, avoiding ballast issues.

name: RMS Titanic
shortDescription: Olympic-class British ocean liner that sank on her maiden voyage after hitting an iceberg.
fullDescription: RMS Titanic was a British ocean liner that sank on 15 April 1912 after striking an iceberg on her maiden voyage from Southampton to New York City. Of the estimated 2,224 passengers and crew aboard, more than 1,500 died, making it one of the deadliest peacetime maritime disasters in history. She was the largest ship afloat at the time of her launching.

name: USS Nimitz (CVN-68)
shortDescription: Lead ship of the Nimitz class — the world's largest warships for decades.
fullDescription: USS Nimitz is a supercarrier and the lead ship of her class. She is powered by two Westinghouse A4W nuclear reactors driving four propeller shafts. The ship can carry 90+ aircraft and has a crew of over 6,000. Nimitz-class carriers have a displacement of over 100,000 tonnes and a flight deck of 332.8 m.

name: German Battleship Bismarck
shortDescription: One of the largest battleships ever built by Germany, sunk in 1941 after just one mission.
fullDescription: Bismarck was the lead ship of her class of battleships built for Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine. She and her sister ship Tirpitz were the largest battleships ever built by Germany. During her only foray into the Atlantic, she sank the British battlecruiser HMS Hood before being hunted down and sunk by the Royal Navy on 27 May 1941, just 8 days after leaving port.

name: M1 Abrams
shortDescription: American main battle tank with a gas turbine engine and composite armour.
fullDescription: The M1 Abrams is a third-generation American main battle tank. It is powered by a Honeywell AGT1500 multi-fuel turbine engine and features advanced composite armour. The M1A2 SEP v3 is the current production variant. The Abrams has seen combat in the Gulf War, Iraq War, and multiple other conflicts.

name: Tiger I
shortDescription: WWII German heavy tank feared for its 88 mm gun and thick armour.
fullDescription: The Tiger I was a German heavy tank of World War II. It was developed in response to the unexpectedly formidable Soviet armour encountered in Operation Barbarossa. The Tiger's 88 mm gun could penetrate any Allied tank at combat ranges, and its 100–120 mm frontal armour was proof against most Allied anti-tank weapons. About 1,347 were produced between 1942 and 1944.

name: Boeing AH-64 Apache
shortDescription: Primary attack helicopter of the US Army, armed with Hellfire missiles and a 30 mm chain gun.
fullDescription: The AH-64 Apache is a four-blade, twin-turboshaft attack helicopter. It features a Target Acquisition Designation Sight and Pilot Night Vision Sensor for all-weather operation. The AH-64E Guardian is the latest variant with improved engines and digital connectivity. It can carry up to 16 AGM-114 Hellfire missiles or 76 unguided rockets.

specs.armament: M230 30mm chain gun, Hellfire missiles, Hydra 70 rockets

name: Northrop Grumman B-2 Spirit
shortDescription: Stealth strategic bomber with a flying wing design and intercontinental range.
fullDescription: The B-2 Spirit is an American stealth heavy strategic bomber with a flying wing design. It is capable of deploying both conventional and thermonuclear weapons. The B-2 has a very low radar cross-section due to its composite materials and smooth shape. Only 21 aircraft were built at a cost of approximately $2.1 billion each.

name: Shinkansen N700 Series
shortDescription: High-speed bullet train operating on Japan's Tokaido and Sanyo Shinkansen lines.
fullDescription: The N700 Series Shinkansen is a high-speed electric multiple unit train operated by JR Central and JR West. It features aerodynamic nose cones, active suspension, and regenerative braking. The N700S variant uses next-generation SiC traction motors and can operate during earthquakes with its onboard battery system.

name: SCMaglev L0 Series
shortDescription: Superconducting maglev train — the fastest land vehicle ever tested at 603 km/h.
fullDescription: The SCMaglev L0 Series is a superconducting magnetic levitation train under development by JR Central. On 21 April 2015, a manned test train reached 603 km/h, setting the world land speed record for a rail vehicle. It uses onboard superconducting electromagnets that interact with coils in the guideway.

name: Ducati Panigale V4
shortDescription: Italian V4-powered superbike derived directly from MotoGP technology.
fullDescription: The Ducati Panigale V4 is a sport motorcycle introduced in 2018. It uses a 1,103cc Desmosedici Stradale V4 engine derived directly from Ducati's MotoGP machine. The bike features a full cornering ABS system, six-axis IMU, and traction control. The V4 R variant produces 221 hp.

name: Kawasaki Ninja H2R
shortDescription: Supercharged track-only motorcycle — the most powerful production motorcycle ever made.
fullDescription: The Kawasaki Ninja H2R is a track-only motorcycle powered by a supercharged 998cc inline-four engine producing 310 hp in ram-air conditions. It was designed by Kawasaki's aerospace, jet engine, and motorcycle divisions. The H2R holds a motorcycle land speed record of 400 km/h and is not street legal.

name: Saturn V
shortDescription: The most powerful rocket ever flown — carried Apollo astronauts to the Moon.
fullDescription: The Saturn V was an American super heavy-lift launch vehicle used by NASA between 1967 and 1973. Developed under Wernher von Braun, it remains the most powerful, heaviest, and largest rocket ever brought to operational status. All 13 Saturn Vs launched successfully, carrying Apollo missions 4 through 17 and the Skylab space station.

name: SpaceX Starship
shortDescription: The largest and most powerful rocket ever built — fully reusable two-stage system.
fullDescription: SpaceX Starship is a fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle. When combined with the Super Heavy booster, it stands 121 m tall and is the largest rocket ever built. Both stages are designed to be fully reusable — the booster is caught by mechanical arms at the launch tower. The system is designed for crewed missions to the Moon and Mars.

specs.type: Fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle

name: Space Shuttle
shortDescription: NASA's partially reusable crewed spacecraft that flew 135 missions from 1981 to 2011.
fullDescription: The Space Shuttle was a partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft system operated by NASA. The system comprised a reusable orbiter, two recoverable solid rocket boosters, and an expendable external tank. It flew 135 missions, launched the Hubble Space Telescope, and served as the primary vehicle for ISS assembly.

name: Caterpillar 797F
shortDescription: One of the largest haul trucks in the world with a 363-tonne payload capacity.
fullDescription: The Caterpillar 797F is an ultra-class off-highway mining truck. With a payload capacity of 363 tonnes, it is among the largest haul trucks ever built. It is powered by a 4,000 hp Cat C175-20 diesel engine. The truck's tyres alone are 4 m tall and cost around $42,500 each. Used primarily in copper and oil sands mines.

name: Liebherr LTM 11200-9.1
shortDescription: The world's most powerful mobile crane with a 1,200-tonne lifting capacity.
fullDescription: The Liebherr LTM 11200-9.1 is a mobile all-terrain crane with a maximum lifting capacity of 1,200 tonnes — the most powerful mobile crane in the world. The main boom can extend to 100 m, and with the luffing jib the maximum hook height reaches 188 m. It has 9 axles and travels at up to 75 km/h.
